title: Introduce row-level security to an existing schema
description: A procedure to add database-enforced tenant isolation incrementally without breaking existing access.
body: Use when hardening a pooled schema that relies only on application scoping. Do not enable broad policies without testing every access path. Steps: ensure each table carries a tenant column; define policies keyed on a session tenant variable; set that variable from the authenticated principal per connection; enable policies in staging and run the full test suite; verify both allowed and denied access. Validation signals include the database denying cross-tenant rows while legitimate access still works. Risk boundary: a misconfigured policy can either leak or lock out tenants, so verify in staging with negative tests before production and gate the rollout.
